title: AVC running systemd in container
body:
On brand-new fresh f29 virt:
```
# podman run --rm --name foo  -ti fedora /usr/sbin/init
systemd 238 running in system mode. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL
+XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=hybrid)
Detected virtualization container-other.
Detected architecture x86-64.

Welcome to Fedora 28 (Twenty Eight)!

Set hostname to <e371dced3e68>.
Failed to install release agent, ignoring: Permission denied
Failed to create /machine.slice/libpod-e371dced3e6855212d79e72b2f569c4cb16a9b91b78a6374883265eebafe36cf.scope/init.scope control group: Permission denied
Failed to allocate manager object: Permission denied
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object, freezing.
Freezing execution.

(hangs here. From another window, /var/log/audit/audit.log):

type=AVC msg=audit(1539652620.118:1546): avc:  denied  { write } for  pid=17729 comm="systemd" name="release_agent" dev="cgroup" ino=7 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c327,c646 tcontext=system_u:object_r:cgroup_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1539652620.118:1547): avc:  denied  { write } for  pid=17729 comm="systemd" name="cgroup.procs" dev="cgroup" ino=819 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c327,c646 tcontext=system_u:object_r:cgroup_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0
type=AVC msg=audit(1539652620.118:1548): avc:  denied  { rmdir } for  pid=17729 comm="systemd" name="init.scope" dev="cgroup" ino=818 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c327,c646 tcontext=system_u:object_r:cgroup_t:s0 tclass=dir permissive=0
```
Running a RHEL container fails differently; details on request, but this one (Fedora) is a simpler failure.

Can reliably reproduce on new f29 virt. On one older f29 virt, with identical podman/runc/selinux*, I cannot reproduce. Could it be that the old system had older versions of podman, and got upgraded, and there's a difference between brand-new-podman and upgraded-podman?? I don't know what else to check. (I've compared `ls -lZR` on `/var/lib/containers`, grep'ing out `container_var_lib_t` and `container_share_t`, and see nothing suspicious.) I'm giving up on this for the day.
```
# rpm -qa|egrep 'podman|runc|selinux'|sort
container-selinux-2.73-2.gitd7a3f33.fc29.noarch
libselinux-2.8-4.fc29.x86_64
libselinux-utils-2.8-4.fc29.x86_64
podman-0.10.1-1.gite4a1553.fc29.x86_64
python2-libselinux-2.8-4.fc29.x86_64
python3-libselinux-2.8-4.fc29.x86_64
rpm-plugin-selinux-4.14.2-1.fc29.x86_64
runc-1.0.0-55.dev.git578fe65.fc29.x86_64
selinux-policy-3.14.2-37.fc29.noarch
selinux-policy-targeted-3.14.2-37.fc29.noarch
```
...and also `oci-systemd-hook-0.1.18-1.git38504cc.fc29`; but the failure is identical whether or not it's installed.

comments.body: Sigh..... yes, that was it. Somewhere in my Ansible cleanup I lost that. Sorry for the false alarm.
